reasoning: On March 1, 2025, Wikipedia listed her rating as 2473. A 1% increase requires her to gain ~25 Elo points (to 2498). For the first resolution date (March 9, 2025), FIDE ratings will not have updated yet (since they update strictly monthly), so Wikipedia will still show 2473, guaranteeing a 'NO'. Thus, March 9 probability is set strictly to the floor. For April 1, she would need to have had a phenomenal over-performance in March to gain 25 points in a single month (with a K-factor of 10, this implies an expected score outperformance of +2.5 in a few weeks), which is quite rare for an established 38-year-old GM. Moreover, Wikipedia often takes a couple of days to update the list, adding further risk. Over longer horizons (May, August, and March 2026), she has more time to accumulate ratings gains by participating in multiple tournaments. Given that her career peak is 2528, a return to ~2498 is historically plausible but would require a prolonged run of excellent form. Probability steadily approaches 0.20 over a 1-year horizon, reflecting typical rating volatility for a player of her caliber.
